profile.name: Md Raz
profile.title: Ph.D. Candidate at NYU
profile.affiliation: New York University, Tandon School of Engineering
profile.researchFocus: Cyber-physical Systems, Trustworthy HW, Low-level HW Design
about: Adaptive and hands-on electrical engineer focused on building impactful systems from concept to prototype.
Experienced in schematic and PCB design, embedded firmware, FPGA/HDL development, and mixed-signal systems.
Research spans cyber-physical security, trustworthy hardware, and digital manufacturing methods for circuits and materials.

projects.name: FEINT Framework
projects.description: Automated framework for insertion of templates and Trojans into FPGA/ASIC RTL targets for attack and defense workflows.

projects.name: OffRAMPS
projects.description: FPGA-based intermediary for observing and modifying additive manufacturing control signals for attack characterization and defense.

projects.name: Ransomware 3.0
projects.description: Research on self-composing and LLM-orchestrated ransomware behavior and mitigation pathways.

research.title: SHIELD: Secure Host-Independent Extensible Logging for Tamper-Proof Detection and Real-Time Mitigation of Ransomware Threats
Offramps: An FPGA-Based Intermediary for Analysis and Modification of Additive Manufacturing Control Systems
FEINT: automated framework for efficient insertion of templates/Trojans into FPGAs
